case_id: Light in Motion: Masterworks of French Impressionism

pi_items.source: task.json specification and setup_task.sh implementation
setup_task.sh creates this file at specified location

Task description specifies 6 major sections: Title page, Curator's Foreword, Exhibition Overview, Catalog Entries, Loan Acknowledgments, Inventory Checklist. At least 5 are appropriate for Heading 1 style.
Exhibition data contains exactly 8 artwork entries (LIM-001 through LIM-008), each requiring a Heading 2 for the artwork title.
setup_task.sh creates exhibition_data.json with all 8 artists. All are verified as real Impressionist painters through web search of their major works.
Wikipedia and museum sources confirm this is Monet's famous 1872 painting at Musée Marmottan Monet that gave the Impressionist movement its name.
Musée Marmottan Monet official museum records and multiple art sources confirm dimensions.

pi_items.metadata_value: Bal du moulin de la Galette
pi_items.verified_value: Bal du moulin de la Galette
pi_items.source: Verified as Renoir's 1876 Impressionist masterpiece at Musée d'Orsay, Paris.
Musée d'Orsay official records and multiple art sources confirm these dimensions.
Verified as Degas' 1874 painting at Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York.
Metropolitan Museum of Art official collection records confirm these dimensions.
Verified as Morisot's 1872 painting at Musée d'Orsay, Paris.
Musée d'Orsay official records and Wikipedia confirm painting measures 46 × 56 cm.
Verified as Caillebotte's 1877 masterpiece at Art Institute of Chicago.
Art Institute of Chicago official records confirm these dimensions.

pi_items.verified_value: The Child's Bath (La Toilette de l'enfant)
pi_items.source: Verified as Cassatt's 1893 painting at Art Institute of Chicago.
Art Institute of Chicago official records confirm these dimensions.

pi_items.verified_value: Boulevard Montmartre at Night (Boulevard Montmartre by Night)
pi_items.source: Verified as Pissarro's 1897 painting at National Gallery, London.

pi_items.verified_value: 64.8 × 53.3 cm (dimensions verified, height × width order may vary)
pi_items.source: National Gallery, London official records and WikiArt confirm painting measures approximately 53.3 × 64.8 cm.
Verified as Sisley's 1872 painting at Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York.
Metropolitan Museum of Art official collection records confirm these dimensions.
All three terms appear in exhibition_data.json: 'Impression' in artwork title, 'Oil on canvas' in all 8 artworks' medium field, 'Catalog' in context of document being created.
Task threshold specification. An ODT file with text content, heading styles, table of contents, and an inventory table should easily exceed 5 KB.

pi_items.metadata_value: Museum registrar at Hartwell Center for American Art
pi_items.source: Task description specifies fictional context with no external data source.
setup_task.sh creates exhibition_data.json with this curator name and foreword text.
setup_task.sh creates exhibition_data.json with these dates as part of synthetic exhibition data.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic exhibition titled 'Light in Motion: Masterworks of French Impressionism' featuring eight real Impressionist paintings: Monet's 'Impression, Sunrise' (1872, Musée Marmottan Monet), Renoir's 'Bal du moulin de la Galette' (1876, Musée d'Orsay), Degas' 'The Dance Class' (1874, Metropolitan Museum), Morisot's 'The Cradle' (1872, Musée d'Orsay), Caillebotte's 'Paris Street; Rainy Day' (1877, Art Institute of Chicago), Cassatt's 'The Child's Bath' (1893, Art Institute of Chicago), Pissarro's 'Boulevard Montmartre at Night' (1897, National Gallery London), and Sisley's 'Bridge at Villeneuve-la-Garenne' (1872, Metropolitan Museum). All artwork dimensions, titles, dates, media (oil on canvas), and lender institutions are factually accurate. The exhibition data is generated by the task's setup script, not sourced from external data. The task requires creating a formatted ODT document with minimum 5 Heading 1 sections and 8 Heading 2 entries for artworks.
